# Jentech Electrical

> Brisbane's premium family-owned electrician. Fully licensed (QLD Electrical Licence 80766), ARC-certified for refrigerant handling (AU068040), trading since incorporation as JENTECH PTY LTD (ABN 46 611 087 462). 5.0-star rating across 158 verified Google reviews. 24/7 emergency service across Greater Brisbane and the Gold Coast hinterland.

## Business Facts

- Legal name: JENTECH PTY LTD
- Trading name: Jentech Electrical
- ABN: 46 611 087 462
- QLD Electrical Licence: 80766
- ARC Refrigerant Handling Licence (RTA): AU068040
- Head office / workshop: 6/190 Station Rd, Yeerongpilly QLD 4105, Australia
- Geographic coordinates: -27.5422, 153.0257
- Phone: 07 3914 9696 (+61 7 3914 9696)
- Email: info@jentechelectrical.com.au
- Standard business hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00am – 4:00pm (Australia/Brisbane time)
- Emergency service availability: 24/7 (including weekends and public holidays)
- Google Business Profile rating: 5.0 / 5.0 across 158 verified customer reviews
- Family-owned: yes
- Dedicated tradesman per commercial client: yes

## Team

[Meet the team](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/team) — the eight people behind Jentech, with named licensed electricians and the office crew that keeps every job moving.

Licensed electricians (5):

- Andrew (Director) — 18 years on the tools. QLD Electrical Worker Licence #129399. Leads the field team, scopes complex jobs, and stays involved through to handover.
- Khydyn (Manager) — 14 years on the tools. QLD Electrical Worker Licence #151153. Specialises in architectural builds, high-end renovations, and any job demanding fine attention to detail.
- Brandon (Team Leader) — 10 years on the tools. QLD Electrical Worker Licence #164332. Coordinates large projects (school buildings), runs ongoing maintenance for school and corporate clients, and leads UPS servicing and installation as a PSS authorised service agent.
- James (Team Leader) — 6 years on the tools. QLD Electrical Worker Licence #179744. Specialises in complex fault-finding, residential renovations, and switchboard upgrades.
- Rowan (Team Leader) — 5 years on the tools. QLD Electrical Worker Licence #1506377. Specialises in commercial fitouts, residential projects, and switchboard upgrades.

Office team (3):

- Karen (Quoting & Accounts) — 30 years across residential and commercial property management plus small building project coordination.
- Michael (Customer Relations) — family history in property investment; first point of contact for many enquiries.
- Rachel (Office Manager) — 6 years residential property management (including leading a property management department), 7 years embedded at Jentech, family background in real estate.

Combined experience: 53 years on the tools across the five licensed electricians; over 90 years of property/business experience across the office team.

## Service Areas

[All service areas](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/service-areas)

Additional suburbs serviced (no dedicated page yet): Brisbane CBD, New Farm, Newstead, Ascot.

## Services

Jentech delivers seven core service lines:

- [Commercial Electrical](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/services/commercial): Fit-outs, emergency breakdowns, repairs and maintenance, testing and compliance. Scheduled compliance programs for hospitality, retail, fitness, medical, office, and industrial tenancies. After-hours work standard to avoid trading disruption.
- [Residential & Renovations](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/services/residential): LED lighting design, full rewiring, switchboard upgrades, new builds, and premium Queenslander renovations. Architect-led collaborations with Inaspace Architecture & Design, grayHAUS, KO&Co Architecture, and Renee Dunn Architect.
- [Industrial](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/services/industrial): 3-phase machinery connections, emergency stops, shop and factory lighting, load balancing, motor control, variable speed drives, power factor correction.
- [Air Conditioning](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/services/air-conditioning): Split system installations (Daikin, Mitsubishi, Fujitsu), multi-split and ducted systems, residential and commercial, year-round servicing. ARC-certified refrigerant handling under Licence AU068040.
- [Smoke Alarms](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/services/smoke-alarms): Installation and compliance testing to AS 3786 and Queensland's 1 January 2027 interconnected-alarm requirements. Photoelectric alarms only (ionisation banned in QLD since 2022). Rental compliance inspections.
- [Switchboards](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/services/switchboards): Upgrades from ceramic-fuse and rewireable boards to modern RCD-protected switchboards. Meter relocations with Energex coordination. Pre-purchase safety inspections. Post-flood remediation.
- [UPS Systems](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/services/ups-systems): Authorised PSS Distributors service agent. NDIS-compliant home UPS installations. Long-life batteries specified for Queensland humidity and sustained high-temperature operation.

## Emergency Response

- [24/7 Emergency Electrician Brisbane](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/emergency-electrician-brisbane): Response for power outages, burning smells, tripping circuit breakers, and after-hours electrical faults. Emergency line answered by licensed electricians (not a third-party call centre).

## Compliance & Standards

Jentech delivers all work to current Australian Standards and Queensland regulations. The specific instruments most often referenced:

- AS/NZS 3000:2018 — Electrical installations (Wiring Rules). The foundation of all compliant electrical work.
- AS/NZS 3008 — Cable sizing and voltage-drop calculations.
- AS/NZS 3012 — Electrical installations for construction and demolition sites.
- AS/NZS 3760 — In-service safety inspection and testing of electrical equipment. Test and tag intervals range from 3 months (construction) to 5 years (low-risk office).
- AS/NZS 2293.1 — Emergency escape lighting and exit signs: design and installation.
- AS/NZS 2293.2 — Emergency escape lighting and exit signs: inspection and maintenance. 6-monthly discharge testing required.
- AS/NZS 4024 — Safety of machinery, including guarding and interlocks.
- AS/NZS 62061 — Functional safety of electrical control systems.
- AS/NZS 5149 — Refrigerating systems and heat pumps: safety and environmental requirements.
- AS/NZS 62040 — Uninterruptible power systems (UPS): performance, safety, and EMC requirements.
- AS 3786:2014 — Smoke alarm product standard. Photoelectric only under QLD law.
- Queensland Electrical Safety Regulation 2013 — 6-monthly push-button RCD testing and 12-monthly operational RCD testing for commercial premises. 5-year record retention.
- Queensland Fire and Emergency Services Act 1990 — smoke alarm compliance obligations.
- Queensland Building Fire Safety Regulation 2008 — emergency lighting log book requirements.
- Work Health and Safety Act 2011 (Qld) — workplace electrical safety duty of care.
- Energex Connection Policy — governs meter relocations and supply upgrades in South East Queensland.

## Key Regulatory Dates

- 1 January 2027 — all Queensland owner-occupied homes must have interconnected photoelectric smoke alarms in every bedroom, every hallway connecting bedrooms, and on every storey.
- Rental properties and sale/lease transactions — already required to be compliant since 2022.
- Ionisation smoke alarms — banned in new installs in Queensland since 2022.

## Featured Projects

Verified client projects with named collaborators:

- [NOFOMO Gym Helensvale](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/nofomo-helensvale): Commercial fit-out, 24/7 member access, smart TVs, neon lighting, nightclub-style sound, full switchboard overhaul.
- [NOFOMO Gym Bundall](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/nofomo-bundall): Full electrical strip-out and rebuild, dynamic lighting, smart TV integration, A/C servicing.
- [Frida's Sip n' Paint Paddington](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/fridas-paddington): Commercial fit-out with boutique lighting, chandeliers, full data and power, back-of-house office.
- [Frida's Sip n' Paint Noosa](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/fridas-noosa): Sunshine Coast commercial fit-out with lighting design.
- [Stoke Green](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/stoke-green): 1930s Queenslander electrical overhaul. Architect: Inaspace Architecture & Design. Builder: grayHAUS.
- [Clementine](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/clementine): 1940s Art Deco residence with period-correct LED retrofit and bespoke chandelier. Designer: grayHAUS.
- [Verney House](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/verney-house): Major Queenslander renovation with heated glass wine cellar, LED downlights, ducted A/C throughout. Builder: grayHAUS.
- [Courtyard House](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/courtyard-house): Three-level residence with integrated alarm, camera, and intercom system. Designer: KO&Co Architecture.
- [Fortitude Valley Queenslander (in progress)](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/fortitude-queenslander): Heritage-listed property with full rewire, LED fittings, rooftop terrace, vehicle turntable. Designer/Builder: grayHAUS.
- [Wilston Redesign (in progress)](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/wilston-redesign): Complete electrical strip-out, switchboard relocation and upgrade. Designer/Builder: grayHAUS.
- [Killara House (in progress)](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects/killara-house): Queenslander with elevator, pool house, made-to-order pendant lighting. Architect: Renee Dunn Architect.

[Full project portfolio](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/projects)

## Guides

Troubleshooting guides for common electrical problems:

- [Loss of Power — Troubleshooting Guide](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/guides/loss-of-power): Safe step-by-step diagnosis of a power outage.
- [No Hot Water — Troubleshooting Guide](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/guides/no-hot-water): Checks to perform before calling a technician or plumber.

## Articles

In-depth content on regulations, pricing context, and Brisbane-specific electrical topics:

- [QLD Smoke Alarm Law 2027 — What Every Homeowner Needs to Know](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/blog/qld-smoke-alarm-2027-deadline-explained): The 1 January 2027 deadline for interconnected photoelectric smoke alarms in every Queensland owner-occupied home. Requirements, history (Slacks Creek fire 2011), and compliance process.
- [Switchboard Upgrade Cost Brisbane 2026 — A Real Price Guide](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/blog/switchboard-upgrade-cost-brisbane): Industry price ranges, what drives the cost, AS/NZS 3000 renovation trigger rule, upgrade-day process.
- [Signs Your Queenslander Needs a Rewire](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/blog/signs-your-queenslander-needs-rewiring): Seven warning signs, VIR/TPS cable service life, heritage preservation during rewire, Council overlay considerations.
- [EV Charger Installation Brisbane — What You Actually Need](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/blog/ev-charger-installation-brisbane): Level 1/2/3 charging explained, single-phase vs 3-phase, load management, coastal spec for river-adjacent homes.
- [Commercial Electrical Compliance in Queensland: A Building Manager's Guide](https://jentechelectrical.com.au/blog/commercial-electrical-compliance-queensland): Six overlapping QLD regulations, record retention requirements, WHSQ and insurer consequences of non-compliance.
